message:
feat(demo): app and test support enhancements

Concurrent agent contributions:
- app.rs: application updates
- test_support.rs: test harness improvements
- Syncs beads tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1bja","title":"charmed_log: Handle RwLock poisoning gracefully instead of panic","description":"# Bug: RwLock Poisoning Causes Application-Wide Crash

## Problem Statement
The charmed_log crate uses .unwrap() on all RwLock read/write acquisitions.
If any thread panics while holding the lock, the lock becomes "poisoned" and
ALL future lock acquisitions will panic, cascading to crash the entire application.

## Affected Code Locations
```
Line 427: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 468: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 475: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 481: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 488: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 494: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 500: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 506: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 512: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 518: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 528: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 579: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 597: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
```

## Root Cause Analysis
The pattern `self.inner.read().unwrap()` assumes the lock will never be poisoned.
However, if a thread panics during:
- Format string processing (e.g., invalid UTF-8)
- Style rendering (e.g., lipgloss issue)
- Any code path holding the lock

Then ALL subsequent logging calls crash with:
`called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: PoisonError { ... }`

## Solution Design
Replace all .unwrap() with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern:

```rust
// Before (panics on poisoned lock):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();

// After (recovers from poisoning):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
```

This pattern:
1. Recovers the inner data even if lock was poisoned
2. Allows logging to continue functioning
3. Is the recommended pattern for "must not fail" operations like logging

## Alternative Considered: Return Result
Changing the API to return Result<(), LogError> was considered but rejected because:
- Breaking API change
- Logging should be fire-and-forget
- Callers shouldn't need to handle logging errors in their business logic

## Implementation Steps
1. Create helper macro or function to encapsulate the unwrap_or_else pattern
2. Replace all 13 occurrences
3. Add test that verifies logging continues after simulated panic
4. Document the recovery behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn logging_continues_after_panic_in_another_thread() {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    
    // Spawn thread that panics while holding lock
    let logger_clone = logger.clone();
    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        // Acquire lock and panic
        // (requires test-only hook to simulate)
    }).join().ok();
    
    // This should NOT panic - logging should continue
    logger.info("After panic", &[]);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 .unwrap() calls replaced with poison-recovery pattern
- [ ] Test demonstrates continued operation after poisoning
- [ ] No performance regression (unwrap_or_else is zero-cost when lock is healthy)
- [ ] Documentation updated to note recovery behavior","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.303042431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.131050035Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.130977760Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - all RwLock accesses use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","panic-fix","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bja","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.329172028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1cgb","title":"EPIC: E2E Test Coverage with Detailed Logging","description":"End-to-end tests for complete application workflows with comprehensive logging.

## Philosophy
- Test full user workflows, not just units
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Capture terminal state at each step
- Support both headless and interactive modes

## Target Applications
1. demo_showcase - primary E2E target
   - All 8 pages (Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings)
   - Navigation flows (sidebar, keyboard shortcuts)
   - Theme switching and persistence
   - Shell-out and restore patterns
   - Wizard deployment workflow

2. glow - markdown viewer
   - File loading and rendering
   - Navigation and search
   - Stash operations

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every test step
- Log all key presses and expected outcomes
- Capture full terminal state on failure
- Include ANSI sequence dumps for debugging
- Log timing information for performance tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.027535577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034727506Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034668586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:41.424387508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:33.366234822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

All 42 app tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:27.187119667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:03:02.843498522Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:03:02.843333565Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-247o","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:27.202846277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-247o","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:16.115608587Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-274o","title":"demo_showcase page: Settings (theme, toggles, about)","description":"A real Settings page that proves the app is configurable, themeable, and polished.

Why:
- Live theme switching is one of the most compelling proofs that lipgloss theming is *real*.
- Toggles (mouse, animations, color mode) demonstrate the stack behaves well across environments (CI/headless, low-color terminals, accessibility).
- A built-in keybindings reference makes the demo usable immediately.

Must showcase:
- Theme picker:
  - preview + apply built-in presets
  - immediate app-wide update (cards, banners, chips, tables, markdown)
- Toggles that apply instantly (no restart):
  - mouse enabled/disabled
  - animations / reduce motion
  - no-color / ASCII mode
  - syntax highlighting (if feature enabled)
  - alt-screen vs no-alt-screen (or at least document this as startup-only if required)
- Keybinding reference:
  - shows global and page-specific shortcuts
  - optionally exposes a customization hook (even if not persisted yet)
- Diagnostics/about:
  - show versions, renderer/color profile, feature flags
  - credits/licenses (brief, tasteful)

Acceptance:
- Every setting has an immediate, visible effect.
- Settings UX is clean: no confusing toggles, and feedback is clear (toast/status message).

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - applying a setting updates the Config/state and triggers expected messages
  - theme switching updates a representative set of themed styles
- E2E:
  - settings toggles + theme switching scenario validates:
    - theme change affects output
    - no-color mode produces expected ASCII output
    - animations toggle changes behavior deterministically

Notes:
- Keep the page small but high-impact: fewer toggles, but each one clearly demonstrates robustness.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.563760533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:42:27.102146644Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","page","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-274o","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:23.577661206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-278q","title":"demo_showcase: realistic demo data + background simulation","description":"Provide believable, dynamic data so the UI feels like a real application.

Deliverables:
- In-memory "backend" that produces:
  - services/projects, environments, deployments, jobs, logs, alerts, docs.
- Deterministic-ish randomness (seedable) so demos are stable but lively.
- Background updates (tick/every + async tasks) that change state over time.

Acceptance:
- UI can show live updates without external network dependencies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.786578236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802547440Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-278q","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802497297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-28co","title":"EPIC: Unit Test Coverage (Real Tests, No Mocks)","description":"Comprehensive unit test coverage for all crates using real implementations.

## Philosophy
- Test actual behavior, not mock behavior
- Use real terminal capabilities where possible
- Test error conditions and edge cases
- Measure coverage and identify gaps

## Target Crates (by priority)
1. bubbletea - core event loop, commands, messages
2. lipgloss - style composition, ANSI output
3. bubbles - component behavior
4. glamour - markdown rendering
5. harmonica - animation calculations
6. huh - form validation and navigation
7. charmed_log - log formatting

## Coverage Targets
- All public functions and methods
- All error paths
- All configuration options
- Edge cases (empty input, large input, unicode)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.152019987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.159363959Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28co","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:28.159323644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28jp","title":"demo_showcase UX: keybindings + interaction model","description":"Define an opinionated interaction model that feels premium:

- Global keys (examples):
  - `?` help overlay, `q` quit, `esc` back/close, `/` search/command palette, `t` theme switcher, `tab` focus next pane.
- Per-widget conventions: list/table navigation, viewport scrolling, form submit/cancel.
- Mouse expectations: click to focus/select; wheel scroll in viewports/lists.

Acceptance:
- A single canonical keymap that pages/components conform to (avoid per-page random keys).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.332580358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185343532Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185278631Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:01.082895581Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.356173194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28l3","title":"Wizard: implement huh form UI embedded in app","description":"Implement the Wizard page using huh components, embedded inside the demo_showcase app chrome.

Why:
- This is the "do the thing" workflow: it makes the demo feel like a real deployment/onboarding tool.
- It showcases huh’s strengths: multi-step forms, validation, confirm/select flows.

Requirements:
- Multi-step flow as designed in `Wizard: design the multi-step workflow`:
  - a mix of input types (text, select, confirm)
  - validations with clear inline errors
- Navigation:
  - cancel/back behavior matches global UX rules (`esc` closes / backs out safely)
  - submit produces a clear success/failure state
- Presentation:
  - keyboard-first
  - mouse optional
  - visually cohesive with the app theme

Integration:
- On completion, the wizard triggers the shared domain action API (separate task) to create a deployment/job and emit logs/toasts.

Acceptance:
- Wizard feels like a real onboarding/deploy tool, not a demo form.
- Validation and recovery flows are polished (errors feel intentional).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.916099726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:14:09.848017766Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:14:09.847943608Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ynf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:41.872484594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:44.500650602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.934899534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:47.373969167Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28wz","title":"Lipgloss: implement partial border edges conformance","description":"Enable the border_partial_top_bottom conformance test by handling partial border edges in run_border_test. The Go API uses BorderTop/BorderBottom/BorderLeft/BorderRight to control which edges render. The Rust Style has these methods; we just need to apply them based on the test name.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.947027377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173555122Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173490171Z","close_reason":"Implemented partial border edges support in lipgloss conformance tests. border_partial_top_bottom now passes. Lipgloss conformance is now 58/58 (was 57/58 with 1 skip).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28wz","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.960863628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mtt","title":"demo_showcase core: command palette (search + actions)","description":"Optional but very compelling polish feature: a command palette ("quick actions") that makes the app feel modern.

Why:
- It dramatically improves discoverability and speed for power users.
- It showcases composing bubbles components (textinput + list) into a cohesive UX.

UX:
- Trigger:
  - `/` (or another agreed key) opens the palette
  - `esc` closes
- Layout:
  - text input at top
  - filtered list of commands below
- Behavior:
  - fuzzy-ish matching over command names + keywords
  - enter runs selected action
  - shows short descriptions and shortcuts (optional)

Actions to include (minimum):
- Navigation:
  - jump to Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
- Toggles:
  - toggle theme picker / cycle theme
  - toggle no-color / reduce-motion
- Utilities:
  - export current view (plain/HTML)
  - open diagnostics (pager / export)

Implementation guidance:
- Maintain a small registry of commands (id, title, keywords, handler).
- Handlers should emit bubbletea Cmds / messages (no direct side effects in UI code).

Acceptance:
- Palette is keyboard-friendly, fast, and feels integrated (not a bolted-on demo).
- Actions are safe in headless/self-check mode (no hangs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.480166189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:37.827452925Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:26.750440223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035096998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.499012063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Polish job progress visualization so it looks like a modern CI dashboard (compelling, readable, information-dense).

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.
- This task should make the Jobs page feel premium even in a plain terminal.

Requirements:
- Use `bubbles::progress` for determinate progress:
  - clear fill, percentage (optional), and label
  - themed colors that match status (running/success/fail/cancel)
- Use spinners for indeterminate states:
  - e.g., “waiting”, “queued”, “uploading”, “finalizing”
- Status clarity:
  - icons/chips for state
  - duration + ETA-ish hint when possible
  - differentiate “stuck” vs “slow” (e.g., warn color after threshold)
- Visual polish:
  - consistent spacing and alignment across rows
  - good behavior in narrow widths (truncate/wrap rules)
  - respects `--no-color` (still readable) and `--no-animations`

Optional (nice if it’s easy):
- Stage-based progress (build/test/deploy) with per-stage completion markers.

Acceptance:
- Looks like a real CI dashboard.

  - wizard -> jobs scenario should assert at least one running job shows an updating progress indicator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:57.767439291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer composing effects with `bubbletea::batch(...)` (toast + log + state refresh) instead of ad-hoc "do one thing" commands.
- Use `bubbletea::sequence(...)` for any multi-step workflow that must occur in order (e.g., release-terminal -> run -> restore, or staged deploy steps).

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700985132Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700919670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ps4","title":"Interactions: enable mouse mode + click/scroll mapping","description":"Enable mouse support in `demo_showcase` and map mouse events to a coherent UX (scroll, click-to-focus/select).

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium signal and makes the showcase feel like a real product.
- It also validates bubbletea’s mouse plumbing (`MouseMsg` parsing + crossterm integration).

Implementation requirements:
- Program configuration:
  - run with mouse enabled (prefer `with_mouse_cell_motion()` to support hover/cell-motion where useful).
  - ensure mouse can be disabled via Settings/Config toggle.
- Scroll mapping:
  - wheel scroll should apply to the *focused pane* (viewport, list, table, file preview).
  - when nothing is explicitly focused, default to the primary content pane.
- Click mapping:
  - click selects within lists/tables/filepicker when coordinates hit rows.
  - click on dashboard cards triggers selection/drill-down.
  - click-to-focus inputs (search bars, text inputs, etc.).

Behavioral constraints:
- Mouse must not fight keyboard navigation:
  - keyboard remains first-class, mouse is additive.
  - selection/focus updates should be consistent regardless of input source.
- Headless/CI safety:
  - mouse support should be a no-op when renderer/terminal features are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll feels natural in viewports/lists/tables/file preview.
- Clicking to select/focus works in the major screens.
- Disabling mouse immediately stops mouse-driven changes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - given `MouseMsg` wheel events, verify the correct component state changes (scroll offset, selection).
  - click hit-testing logic for at least one list/table and one card grid.
- E2E (optional but preferred):
  - use the harness “raw byte” mode to inject xterm mouse sequences and assert state changes in artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep hit-testing deterministic: base calculations on known layout metrics (width/height + fixed styles).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:33:42.174740025Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:33:42.174598581Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:01.714323165Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-665u","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage Suite","description":"Master epic for achieving comprehensive test coverage across all charmed_rust crates.

## Goals
- Real tests only - NO mocks, fakes, or stubs
- Direct integration with actual terminal/TTY behavior
- Property-based testing for algorithmic/numeric code
- E2E tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Sub-Epics
1. Unit Test Coverage - expand tests in every crate
2. Integration Test Coverage - cross-crate and terminal tests
3. E2E Test Coverage - full application workflows
4. Property Test Coverage - proptest for edge cases

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have at least one test
- Integration tests verify cross-crate behavior
- E2E tests cover critical user workflows
- Property tests catch edge cases in numeric/algorithmic code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100709336Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7jxu","title":"demo_showcase theme: polished UI components (tabs, chips, banners)","description":"Build higher-level styled UI primitives so pages can be assembled like a design system.

Why:
- The demo needs a cohesive, premium visual language.
- Centralizing these patterns prevents ad-hoc styling and keeps pages consistent.

Components to provide (examples):
- Tabs/pills for sub-sections
- Chips/tags for status (ok/warn/error/running)
- Alert banners (info/warn/error) with icons and optional actions
- Empty states (icon + title + guidance)
- Progress-with-label layouts
- Small "stat" widgets (label + value + delta)

Design constraints:
- Must work in:
  - truecolor and 256-color
  - no-color/ASCII mode (fallback styles)
  - narrow terminals (components truncate gracefully)
- Components should be usable from any page with minimal configuration.

Testability:
- Components should be snapshot-testable (render a small representative example set).

Acceptance:
- Pages can assemble these like a cohesive design system.
- UI feels consistent and premium across Dashboard/Jobs/Logs/Docs/Wizard.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.396250192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479515924Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479451744Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.411568884Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:34.170348451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688983950Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mwty","title":"Logs: implement live log viewer (viewport + follow mode)","description":"Implement the Logs page core UI (the thing users will stare at for minutes).

Why:
- Logs are the heartbeat of an ops console.
- This is where styling + performance + interaction quality are most obvious.

Requirements:
- Viewport that can scroll back through a large buffer.
- Follow mode (tail -f) toggle:
  - when enabled, viewport stays pinned to latest entries
  - when user scrolls up, follow pauses automatically
  - a clear UI hint indicates "paused" vs "following"
- Selection (optional but great):
  - highlight current line for copy/export

Performance:
- Must handle thousands of lines without flicker.
- Avoid rebuilding huge strings unnecessarily; prefer rendering only the visible window.

Integration:
- Uses structured log entries + formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`).
- Filtering/search is layered on later but the core viewer should support it cleanly.

Acceptance:
- Smooth scrolling and no flicker.
- Follow mode behavior matches user expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.085412578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.090025763Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.089960261Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogsPage with live log viewer: viewport scrolling, follow mode (tail -f), auto-pause on scroll up, status bar with level counts and position. +{"id":"bd-ndn1","title":"EPIC: Property-Based Testing (proptest)","description":"Property-based testing using proptest for algorithmic and numeric code.

## Philosophy
- Let the computer find edge cases humans miss
- Test invariants, not specific inputs
- Shrink failing cases to minimal reproductions
- Focus on numeric/algorithmic code

## Target Areas
1. harmonica - spring physics calculations
   - Animation value convergence
   - Velocity damping behavior
   - Frame timing consistency

2. lipgloss - dimension calculations
   - Width/height calculations with padding/margin
   - Position calculations
   - Unicode width handling

3. bubbles - component state machines
   - Viewport scroll bounds
   - Progress bar percentages
   - Spinner frame cycling

4. glamour - text wrapping
   - Word wrap boundaries
   - Unicode character handling
   - ANSI sequence preservation

## Property Examples
- For any input, width >= content width
- Animation always converges to target
- Scroll position always in valid range
- Progress percentage always 0-100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.367379778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381961284Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381903246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-nw3u","title":"Fix run_conformance JSON timestamp (use real RFC3339)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:44:06.445942217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358436941Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358359595Z","close_reason":"Fixed: chrono_lite_timestamp() now uses chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339_opts() for real RFC3339 timestamps","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-pfqw","title":"demo_showcase: e2e test harness + scripts","description":"Build comprehensive end-to-end verification for the demo_showcase binary.

Why:
- The showcase is the "integration demo" for the whole workspace; it must be reliably testable.
- We want confidence that *everything still works* after refactors.

Constraints:
- E2E must run in CI without a real TTY.
- Avoid flaky sleeps/timeouts.

Strategy:
- Use bubbletea custom I/O + deterministic tick driving.
- Use a scripted input runner to navigate the UI and trigger actions.
- Capture output frames and structured logs as artifacts.

Definition of done:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` runs E2E scenarios quickly (<~10s) and deterministically.
- On failure, logs clearly explain which scenario/step failed and why.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.143197913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237604388Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pfqw","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237574673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qkxb","title":"Dashboard: drill-down details modal/panel","description":"Add an interaction to open a drill-down details view for a selected service/deployment/job directly from the Dashboard.

Why:
- This is a classic "real product" interaction: overview → detail without losing context.
- It also exercises multi-level navigation, focus trapping, and modal/panel layout.

Design options (pick one and be consistent):
- Modal overlay (centered) with a dimmed background, OR
- Side panel (right) that can be toggled open/closed

Content to show:
- Entity summary (name, status, last update)
- Key metrics (recent latency/error rate)
- Recent events/log snippets (a few lines)
- Action shortcuts (navigate to Logs/Jobs filtered to this entity)

Interaction requirements:
- Keyboard-first:
  - `enter`/`space` opens details for selected card
  - `esc` closes
  - focus stays consistent and returns to previous card

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates multi-level navigation without leaving the dashboard.
- Looks polished and does not break layout on smaller terminals.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.222675219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:48.487450193Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:23.173008139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.238425251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:21.961442429Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
